questions.question: Explain the difference between the SIMD and MIMD architectural classifications in Flynn's Taxonomy. Provide a real-world example of hardware for each.
expectedPoints: SIMD: Single Instruction operates on Multiple Data elements simultaneously (e.g., GPU).
MIMD: Multiple Instructions operate on Multiple Data streams concurrently (e.g., multicore CPU cluster).
Contrast their control mechanism (centralized vs. decentralized).

questions.question: Compare and contrast shared-memory and distributed-memory parallel architectures. Your answer should include their key characteristics, advantages, disadvantages, and a typical programming model used for each.
expectedPoints: Definition: Logical vs. Physical memory organization.
Key Advantage of Shared Memory: Ease of programming (single address space).
Key Advantage of Distributed Memory: Scalability and absence of memory contention.
Key Disadvantage of Shared Memory: Memory bandwidth bottleneck and cache coherence overhead.
Key Disadvantage of Distributed Memory: Complexity of explicit communication (message passing).
Programming Models: OpenMP/Pthreads for shared memory; MPI for distributed memory.
